revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23985667504|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:東乗鞍古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Higashinorikura Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|location={{ill|Tenri|qid=Q502196|1=Tenri, Nara}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.585194444444|N|135.84736944444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}|part_of={{ill|Somanouchi Kofun Cluster|qid=Q11523653|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}}}} {{Infobox Japanese Kofun |name = Higashinorikura Kofun |image = [[File:Higashinorikura Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|280px]]
Mound
(Rear circular portion and stone chamber opening on the right, front rectangular portion in the left background) |alt_name = |group = [[Somanouchi Kofun Group]] |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}, {{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|qid=Q502196|lt=Tenri City|1=Tenri, Nara}}, Somanouchi Town, Higashinorikura area & Otsugi Town, Kitayama area{{Sfn|Higashinorikura Kofun (Tenri City Historic Site)|1990}} |coordinates = 34 |coordinates = 35 |coordinates = 6.70 |coordinates = 135 |coordinates = 50 |coordinates = 50.53 |omitted = JP-29 |type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} |size = Mound length: 83 meters |burial = Right-side-sleeve {{ill|Corridor-type stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-type stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}
(One hollowed house-shaped Sarcophagus, one assembled Sarcophagus) |burial goods = {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}, reported armor plates and horse fittings |associated_mounds = |built = Early 6th century |buried_person = |imperial_tomb = |historic_site = None |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = Japan Nara |type = Keyhole-shaped Kofun - WNW |map_dot_label = Higashinorikura Kofun |map_dot_label = top }} '''Higashinorikura Kofun''' (東乗鞍古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|qid=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} Kofun located in {{ill|Tenri, Nara|en|Tenri, Nara|qid=Q502196|lt=Tenri City|1=Tenri, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}, Japan. It is a keyhole-shaped Kofun (前方後円墳) and one of the mounds that make up the {{ill|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|en|Somanouchi Kofun cluster|qid=Q11523653|lt=Somanouchi Kofun cluster|1=Somanouchi Kofun cluster}}. The site has not been Historic Site1 as a historic landmark. == Overview == This kofun is situated along the ridgeline of a hill extending east to west on the eastern edge of the Nara Basin. Together with mounds such as the {{ill|Nishinorikura Kofun|ja|西乗鞍古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinorikura Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Obaka Kofun|lt=Obaka Kofun|lt=Kobaka Kofun|ja|小墓古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobaka Kofun|qq=}}, it types part of a subgroup within the Somanouchi Kofun Group. Multiple excavations have been conducted at this site. The mound is characterized by its well-developed rectangular front portion, oriented toward the west-northwest. The mound structure has two tiers. Its previously estimated length of 75 meters{{Sfn|Higashinorikura Kofun (Heibonsha)|2006}}{{Sfn|Higashinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}} was revised to 83 meters following recent investigations.[https://www.nara-np.co.jp/news/20200225090436.html "Mound Length Confirmed to Be 83 Meters - 10 Meters Larger Than Expected"] (Nara Newspaper, February 25, 2020). Fragments of {{ill|cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}} were detected on the surface, and a surrounding moat approximately 10 meters wide and 2 meters deep was identified on the western side of the rectangular portion.{{Sfn|Higashinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}} The burial chamber is located in the rear circular portion and features a large right-side-sleeve {{ill|corridor-type stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-type stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} measuring 14.6 meters in total length, constructed using massive stones. The chamber opens to the south and houses two coffins: a hollowed house-shaped Sarcophagus made of Aso welded tuff (also known as Aso pink stone or Mamon stone) and an assembled Sarcophagus made of white tuff from {{ill|Futakami Mountain|ja|二上山|1=Futakami Mountain|2=ja|3=二上山|lt=Mount Futakami (or Nijo)|comment=Kanji are ambiguous between the two pronounciations|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|13=User:Immanuelle/Futakami Mountain|qq=}}.{{Sfn|Higashinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}} The chamber was looted, but burial goods such as armor plates and horse fittings were reportedly unearthed, although their current whereabouts are unknown.{{Sfn|Higashinorikura Kofun (Tenri Kofun 100)|2015}} The mound is believed to date to the early 6th century, during the early {{ill|late Kofun period|ja|古墳時代後期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/late Kofun period|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 古墳時代|1=Kofun period|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q459939}}. Nearby, Nishinorikura Kofun and Kobaka Kofun, dating to the late mid-Kofun to early late-Kofun periods, are also known, and these are hypothesized to be the Kofuns of chiefs from the {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|Mononobe clan|qid=Q1755836|lt=Mononobe clan|1=Mononobe clan}}.{{Cite book|和書|author=Shiraishi Taichirō|chapter=The Mononobe Clan Viewed from Kofun|title=Reasoning About the Buried of Kofun (Chūkō Series)|publisher=[[Chūōkōron-shinsha]]|year=2018|pages=253-260}} == burial == The burial facilities include a right-side-sleeve corridor-type stone chamber located in the rear circular portion of the mound. The chamber opens southward and has the following area:{{Sfn|Higashinorikura Kofun (Heibonsha)|1981}}{{Sfn|Higashinorikura Kofun (Kofun Encyclopedia)|1989}} * Total chamber length: 14.6 meters * Burial chamber (gen-shitsu): Length approx. 7.6 meters, width approx. 2.4 meters, current height approx. 3.3 meters * Passageway (sen-dō): Length approx. 7 meters, width approx. 1.7 meters, current height approx. 1.5 meters The chamber was constructed using massive natural stones of granite. The rear wall comprises three stacked layers. Inside the burial chamber: - Toward the rear wall, a hollowed house-shaped Sarcophagus made of Aso welded tuff is placed, characterized by its narrow flat top and rope loop protrusions. - Toward the passageway, an assembled Sarcophagus made of white tuff from Futakami Mountain is located, though only the base remains. == See Also == * [[Aso Pink Stone]] * [[Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|(Sources used for article creation)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=Higashinorikura Kofun|title=Tenri City Historic Site|publisher=Tenri City Board of Education|ref={{Harvid|Higashinorikura Kofun (Tenri City Historic Site)|1990}}}} == External Links == * [http://www.city.tenri.nara.jp/kakuka/kyouikuiinkai/bunkazaika/kohun/1445321339401.html Higashinorikura Kofun] - Tenri City Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|東乗鞍古墳|version=99995326|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Higashinorikura Kofun}} [[ja:東乗鞍古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q80951331}} [[Category:History of Tenri, Nara]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Mononobe clan]] [[Category:Mononobe clan|Burial Higashinorikura Kofun]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Somanouchi Kofun cluster]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]
